name: Giant Oceanic Manta Ray
summary: Giant oceanic manta rays are huge, graceful rays that glide through warm seas using wing-like fins. They filter tiny prey from the water, visit reef cleaning stations, and are the largest rays in the world.
metaTitle: Manta Ray Facts | Ocean Habitat, Diet & Photos
metaDescription: Discover giant oceanic manta ray facts with photos, ocean habitat, plankton diet, pups, migration, and conservation.
searchIntents: how big is a manta ray
speed: Powerful gliding swimmer in open water
funFacts: Manta rays have the largest brain-to-body ratio of any fish.
Their cephalic lobes help scoop food-rich water into the mouth.
Some manta rays leap fully out of the sea in a behavior called breaching.
They often gather at reef cleaning stations where small fish remove parasites.
A newborn manta pup can already have a wingspan of about 4 to 5 feet.
Manta rays do not have a stinging tail like some other rays.

faq.question: What is a manta ray?
faq.answer: A manta ray is a giant ray with broad fins that make it look like it is flying underwater. This page focuses on the giant oceanic manta ray.

faq.question: Where do manta rays live?
faq.answer: Giant oceanic manta rays live in warm oceans around the world, especially in tropical and subtropical seas.

faq.question: What do manta rays eat?
faq.answer: They eat tiny animals such as zooplankton, krill, and small fish by filtering seawater through their gills.

faq.question: Do manta rays sting people?
faq.answer: No. Manta rays do not have a venomous tail spine, and they are not dangerous to swimmers.

faq.question: How big is a giant oceanic manta ray?
faq.answer: Very large adults can span more than 20 feet from fin tip to fin tip and weigh several thousand pounds.

faq.question: Are manta rays endangered?
faq.answer: Yes. Giant oceanic manta rays are listed as Endangered because they reproduce slowly and are threatened by fishing, bycatch, and ocean pollution.
